# graft — Full LLM Summary

graft is a Go CLI that keeps one canonical, provider-neutral agent definition in sync across eight AI-coding providers: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, OpenCode, Roo Code, Goose, and Grok CLI. The canonical store under .graft/ is the single source of truth. Sync is bidirectional: a change in any provider is pulled back to canonical and pushed out to the rest. One planned provider (Antigravity) is in the catalog but not yet built into the sync engine, pending a research spike on its agent-definition format. One provider (Gemini CLI) is deprecated — previously a supported, active provider, removed from the active set on 2026-06-15 and kept in code and catalog as reference.

## The canonical-first model

graft has one job: keep a single canonical agent definition equal to its copies across every provider. Edit an agent once; graft propagates that change to every provider automatically. Pull a change made in any single provider back to the canonical source and out to the rest.

The canonical store lives at .graft/agents/<name>/ in your workspace root and holds:
- agent.yaml: canonical fields (name, description, model, tools, mcp, permissions, providerOverrides)
- instructions.md: the agent's body / system prompt
- .meta.json: per-provider source hash and last commit hash for drift detection

## The eight active providers

| Provider ID | Tool | Skills support |
|---|---|---|
| claude-code | Claude Code | Yes |
| codex | Codex | Yes |
| cursor | Cursor | No |
| github-copilot | GitHub Copilot | No |
| opencode | OpenCode | Yes |
| roo-code | Roo Code | No |
| goose | Goose | No |
| grok-cli | Grok CLI | No |

Each provider implements the same interface: Detect (find agent files), Parse (read into provider form), ToCanonical (map to canonical), Serialize (render back to provider format), and Schema (provide JSON Schema for validation).

Skills are self-contained capability directories. Three providers support skills: Claude Code (.claude/skills/), Codex (native canonical discovery of .agents/skills/), and OpenCode (.opencode/skills/). Skills are stored once in .agents/skills/ (canonical) and symlinked — not copied — into each supporting provider.

## Provider overrides

Providers carry settings with no neutral home in the canonical model. These are stored under providerOverrides[<provider_id>] in agent.yaml and restored verbatim when serializing back to that provider. The agent's name is never overridable — it is the agent's identity. Unknown provider keys in providerOverrides are errors; graft uses Levenshtein distance to suggest the nearest valid provider id.

## Planned providers

A planned provider is in the catalog but not yet built into the sync engine:

- **Antigravity**: Has a catalog entry but is unregistered in the sync engine pending a research spike on the agent-definition format. It has never been active.

## Deprecated providers

A deprecated provider was previously active but has been removed from the active set; its code and catalog entry are kept for reference and should not be used:

- **Gemini CLI**: Previously a supported, active provider; deprecated on 2026-06-15 and removed from the active set (unregistered from the sync and skills engines). Its catalog entry is marked `"deprecated": true` and it is excluded from sync, agent, and skill operations. This is distinct from a planned provider like Antigravity, which was never active.

## How sync works

1. **Detect & diff**: Detect working tree changes vs base (current branch).
2. **Branch per file**: Create graft/<run_id>/agent/<name> for each changed agent.
3. **Canonicalize**: Transform each changed provider file into canonical form and update .graft/agents/<name>/.
4. **Merge loop**: Merge per-agent branches sequentially into a moving beta branch (graft/<run_id>/beta/<n>).
5. **Conflict handling**: If merge conflicts arise, graft records the path, surfaces it, and stops (resumable). Manually resolve, then run graft sync --continue to resume from the recorded phase.
6. **Reapply to moving base**: If the base branch moved during the run, redo the merge onto the new base; otherwise proceed.
7. **Serialize to providers**: Copy the stabilized canonical tree into working dir and serialize it out to every enabled provider. Prune temporary branches.
8. **No commit to base**: graft never commits to your base branch; the beta branch is only a tracked reference.

Resumability: The run row stores phase and beta_branch in sqlite for resuming interrupted syncs. Concurrency: An exclusive lock per workspace (root, remote, branch) ensures only one sync runs at a time; a second sync waits or resumes. One global sqlite database with WAL and lock support.

## Drift detection and status

Drift is when a provider's agent file no longer matches what graft expects. For each provider, graft stores a content hash (PROVIDER_LINK). The status command recomputes the provider file hash and marks in_sync only when it matches both the stored hash and the canonical. Otherwise, it is marked as drifted.

StatusReport surfaces:
- agents[]: per-provider in/out-of-sync status
- out_of_sync_providers: map of provider → count of drifted agents

Status commands are read-only and non-destructive.

## Validation

graft validates schema and semantics before every sync. Validation runs the Validator on the changed scope and returns findings (agent, provider, path, message, severity). Errors block sync. Warnings never block but are reported.

## Configuration

Global and per-project configuration control graft's behavior:
- sync.gitAuto (bool): auto-commit tracking branches vs builtin-git only.
- scope (string, default "agents"): which capability is synced (agents, skills planned).
- providers.enabled[] ([]string): subset of the eight providers participating in sync when mode=specific.
- providers.disabled[] ([]string): subset of the eight providers excluded from sync when mode=all.
- providers.mode (string): "all" (all except disabled) or "specific" (only enabled) — controls which providers to include.

Read and write config with `graft config get` and `graft config set`.

## CLI commands

- **graft init**: Create .graft/, register workspace, detect git mode. Output: created path.
- **graft agent list**: List canonical agents and provider coverage. Output: table.
- **graft agent <name> status**: Show drift of one agent across providers.
- **graft agent init <name> [prompt]**: Scaffold a new canonical agent. Gives it a non-empty default description ("<name> agent") and auto-syncs it to detected providers unless --no-sync is passed. Output: agent record + sync result.
- **graft agent model <name>**: Set or clear a per-provider model override. Flags: --provider (required), --model, --clear.
- **graft agent sync [<name>]**: Alias for `graft sync agents` (no arg) or `graft sync agent <name>` (with arg).
- **graft agents status**: Aggregated drift: out-of-sync providers and agent counts.
- **graft sync agent <name>**: Sync one agent. Flags: --dry-run, --continue. Output: run_id and result.
- **graft sync agents**: Sync all changed agents. Flags: --dry-run, --continue, --ingest.
- **graft validate**: Schema + semantic validation; non-zero exit on errors. Flags: --all, --provider <id>.
- **graft skill install <name|path>**: Copy a skill into the canonical .agents/skills/ directory and symlink into providers.
- **graft skill list**: List canonical skills and their symlink status in each provider.
- **graft skill status**: Show per-provider link state for each canonical skill. Flags: --provider, --override.
- **graft skill sync**: Create or refresh symlinks from .agents/skills/ into each supporting provider.
- **graft config get**: Print resolved config (project over global). Use -g for global only.
- **graft config set**: Write config keys using flags (e.g., --scope agents, --providers.mode specific).
- **graft catalog verify**: Verify embedded catalog hashes match the manifest. Output: OK / FAIL.
- **graft destroy**: Remove graft's managed state. Provider agent files are never touched.
- **graft update**: Check for / install a newer graft release. Flags: --check.

## Network endpoints

Outbound endpoints graft contacts at runtime:
- GitHub: api.github.com (for release checks and provider schema fetches)
- Custom agent catalog endpoints (configuration-dependent)

For air-gapped or firewall-restricted environments, allowlist these endpoints.

## Getting started

1. [Quickstart](/getting-started/quickstart): Install graft and sync your first agent — shortest path, under 5 minutes.
2. [Install](/getting-started/install): Detailed install, verify, upgrade, and uninstall instructions.
3. [Core concepts](/concepts/overview): The canonical-first mental model and vocabulary.
4. [CLI reference](/reference/cli): Full reference for all commands and flags.
5. [Config reference](/reference/config): All configuration keys and defaults.
6. [Canonical format](/reference/canonical-format): Complete reference for agent.yaml fields.
7. [FAQ & troubleshooting](/reference/faq): Answers to common questions.

## Skills (supported on 3 providers)

Skills are self-contained capability directories read by the supporting tool at startup. They are stored once in a canonical location (.agents/skills/) and symlinked — not copied or merged — into each supporting provider's skills folder. This keeps them in sync without duplication.

Supported providers:
- Claude Code: .claude/skills/
- Codex: native canonical discovery of .agents/skills/
- OpenCode: .opencode/skills/

Manage skills with:
- `graft skill install <name|path>`: Copy a skill into the canonical store and symlink into providers.
- `graft skill list`: List canonical skills and symlink status.
- `graft skill sync`: Create or refresh symlinks in each supporting provider.

## Providers overview

Each provider has:
- **Name**: A stable, lowercase id (claude-code, codex, etc.).
- **Detect**: Find this provider's agent files under the workspace root.
- **Parse**: Read a provider file into provider-shaped form.
- **ToCanonical**: Map a parsed provider agent into canonical form, preserving non-canonical fields under providerOverrides.
- **Serialize**: Render a canonical agent into this provider's file(s), restoring providerOverrides.
- **Schema**: Return the provider's JSON Schema for validation.

The sync engine treats all providers uniformly because they all implement the same interface.

## Supported platforms

macOS and Windows are both supported. On Windows, symlink-based skill linking requires Developer Mode or Administrator privileges. Without those, skill commands report a capability error rather than silently falling back to file copy.

## What is not active yet

- No TUI or web UI.
- No git hooks: migration and sync run only when you invoke `graft sync`.
- Antigravity is planned but not yet wired into the sync engine; Gemini CLI is deprecated (previously active, removed from the active set).
